primary_sources.observed: When a selected sink becomes unavailable, the user agent takes no action; playback may continue logically while audio is not rendered. Applications may observe devicechange and re-enumerate outputs.

primary_sources.status: W3C Web Audio API 1.1 editor draft dated 2026-06-12
primary_sources.observed: AudioContext routing state is browser-reported state and must remain separate from physical audibility evidence.

finding: A selected output can disappear without pausing media or producing an automatic fallback, creating a silent-but-logically-playing state. A privacy-preserving evaluator can classify this transition without retaining device IDs or labels.
privacy_boundary: No deviceId, groupId, label, permission payload, or raw enumerateDevices result is retained.
trust_limit: The evaluator classifies browser-observed routing transitions only. It does not prove that sound was audible, that a device physically existed, or that a user heard output.
falsification_route: Run on a physical browser with a permitted non-default sink, unplug or disconnect it during playback, capture devicechange plus permitted output-presence booleans, and compare the classification with independent physical audibility evidence.
